Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema example extends extensions link metadata presentation purpose rich-text status component pattern style theme token accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle scale state variant

4 definitions in this file:

  • tokenValue
  • tokenApi
  • token
  • tokenGroup
 The resolved value as a human-readable string (e.g., '#1a1a1a', '16px', '400', 'rgb(0, 85, 179)'). If this token is an alias, the name of the referenced token (e.g., 'color.neutral.900', 'spacing.base'). Corresponds to the W3C Design Tokens Format Module alias/reference concept (§3.8, §7): a token whose value references another token rather than containing an explicit value. How the token's value was derived, using terminology from the W3C Design Tokens Format Module (§3.8, §7). 'explicit' means the token has a direct, standalone value. 'alias' means the value references another token (identified in the `reference` property). When omitted, tools SHOULD infer: if `reference` is present the token is an alias, otherwise it is explicit. URI or relative path to the W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) file containing the authoritative value. [ { "resolved": "#1a1a1a", "reference": "color.neutral.900", "dtcgFile": "./tokens/color.tokens.json" }, { "resolved": "16px", "reference": "spacing.base", "dtcgFile": "./tokens/spacing.tokens.json" }, { "resolved": "#0055b3" }, { "resolved": "400", "reference": "font.weight.regular" }, { "resolved": "0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12)", "reference": "shadow.elevation.low", "dtcgFile": "./tokens/elevation.tokens.json" } ]

Open map: keys are strings, values are string

[ { "cssCustomProperty": "--color-text-primary", "scssVariable": "$color-text-primary", "lessVariable": "@color-text-primary", "jsConstant": "colorTextPrimary", "iosToken": "Color.Text.primary", "androidToken": "colorTextPrimary", "designToolVariable": "color/text/primary", "composeToken": "Color.Text.Primary", "flutterConstant": "colorTextPrimary", "tailwindClass": "text-primary" }, { "cssCustomProperty": "--space-4", "scssVariable": "$space-4", "jsConstant": "space4" }, { "cssCustomProperty": "--font-weight-bold", "jsConstant": "fontWeightBold", "iosToken": "Font.Weight.bold", "androidToken": "fontWeightBold", "swiftUIFont": "Font.Weight.bold", "composeToken": "Typography.Weight.Bold" } ] Identifies this entity as a token. The token name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). Also serves as the human-readable display name. The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow'). Declares that this token inherits from a base token in a parent design system. The base token provides the foundational value, type, API mappings, and guidelines. This token layers on top — overriding the value, adding platform API mappings, or providing more guidelines while inheriting everything else. Alternative names this token is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing. A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup. A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards. A description of the token — what it represents, where to use it, and any constraints. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the token name and type. A visual or interactive preview — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type from the presentation schema. Lifecycle status of the token. Optional. When omitted inside a token group, inherited from the parent group. When omitted at the top level, tools SHOULD treat the token as having no declared status. The version in which this token was introduced. Freeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation'). The token's semantic category. Standard values: 'base' (raw palette values, not used directly in components), 'semantic' (purpose-driven tokens that map to base values, e.g., 'color-text-primary'), 'component' (tokens scoped to a specific component, e.g., 'button-background'). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case. All structured docs for this token as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Tokens accept general guideline types only (best-practices, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each guideline has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. External resource links, related artifact references, and internal relationships. Each link can point to an external URL, an internal DSDS artifact by name, or both. References: entityExtends, presentationImage, richText, presentationVideo, presentationCode, presentationUrl, status, tokenValue, tokenApi, tokenGuideline, link, extensions { "kind": "token", "name": "color-text-primary", "summary": "Default body text color for light and dark surfaces.", "description": "The default color for body text, headings, and labels. Provides the highest-contrast text color for standard reading content on default background surfaces.", "status": { "overall": "stable", "platforms": { "css": { "status": "stable", "since": "2.0.0" }, "ios": { "status": "stable", "since": "2.1.0" }, "android": { "status": "stable", "since": "2.2.0" }, "figma": { "status": "stable", "since": "2.0.0" } } }, "since": "2.0.0", "tags": [ "color", "text", "body" ], "tokenType": "color", "category": "semantic", "value": { "resolved": "#1a1a1a", "reference": "color.neutral.900", "resolution": "alias", "dtcgFile": "./tokens/color.tokens.json" }, "aliases": [ "color.text.default", "color.text.body" ], "api": { "cssCustomProperty": "--color-text-primary", "scssVariable": "$color-text-primary", "jsConstant": "colorTextPrimary", "iosToken": "Color.Text.primary", "androidToken": "colorTextPrimary", "designToolVariable": "color/text/primary" }, "guidelines": [ { "kind": "purpose", "useCases": [ { "description": "When applying color to body text, headings, and form labels on default background surfaces.", "kind": "positive" }, { "description": "When building components that display primary reading content that must adapt across color modes.", "kind": "positive" }, { "description": "When placing text on dark or colored background surfaces.", "kind": "negative", "alternative": { "name": "color-text-inverse", "rationale": "This token is optimized for contrast against light backgrounds. Using it on dark or saturated surfaces will fail contrast requirements." } }, { "description": "When the text is inside a component that supplies its own scoped color tokens (e.g., text on a filled button).", "kind": "negative", "alternative": { "name": "color-text-on-action", "rationale": "Component-scoped tokens account for the specific background they sit on. Applying a general text token to a scoped context risks contrast failures." } } ] }, { "kind": "best-practices", "items": [ { "guidance": "Use for all body text, headings, and form labels on default background surfaces.", "rationale": "A single primary text color ensures visual consistency and meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements against the system's default background.", "kind": "encouraged", "category": "visual-design" }, { "guidance": "Do not override this token's value at the component level. Use color-text-secondary or color-text-tertiary for reduced emphasis.", "rationale": "Overriding the primary text color creates inconsistency. The system provides lower-emphasis text tokens for visual hierarchy.", "kind": "prohibited", "category": "visual-design" }, { "guidance": "This color meets a 15.3:1 contrast ratio against color-background-default in light mode.", "rationale": "Exceeds WCAG 2.1 AAA requirements (7:1 for normal text), ensuring readability for users with low vision.", "kind": "informational", "category": "accessibility", "criteria": [ { "url": "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum" }, { "url": "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-enhanced" } ] } ] }, { "kind": "accessibility", "wcagLevel": "AAA", "colorContrast": [ { "foreground": "color-text-primary", "background": "color-background-default", "contrastRatio": 15.3, "level": "AAA", "context": "Primary text on default background in light mode." }, { "foreground": "color-text-primary", "background": "color-background-subtle", "contrastRatio": 13.8, "level": "AAA", "context": "Primary text on subtle background (card surface) in light mode." } ] } ], "links": [ { "kind": "source", "url": "https://code.acme.com/design-system/src/tokens/color/text.tokens.json", "label": "Token source file" }, { "kind": "documentation", "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-primary", "label": "Token documentation" }, { "kind": "related", "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-secondary", "label": "color-text-secondary" }, { "kind": "related", "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-tertiary", "label": "color-text-tertiary" } ] } Identifies this entity as a token-group. The token group name. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). Also serves as the human-readable display name. Alternative names this token group is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing. A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup. A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards. A description of the group's purpose, the tokens it contains, and how they relate. Optional; when omitted, tools MAY generate a description from the group name and children. A visual or interactive preview — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type from the presentation schema. Lifecycle status of the token group. Optional. When omitted inside a parent token group, inherited from the parent. Children that omit their own status inherit this group's status. The version in which this group was introduced. Freeform keywords for categorization and search (e.g., 'color', 'palette', 'semantic'). When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here to avoid repetition on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'. When all tokens in this group share the same semantic category, declare it here. Standard values: 'base', 'semantic', 'component'. Each token in children MAY override this value. An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups in this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve order for display, as it often represents a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest). Children inherit tokenType, category, and status from this group when they omit their own values.
Min items: 1 All structured docs for this token group as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Accepts general guideline types only (best-practices, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Guidelines for the group as a whole live here. Guidelines for individual tokens SHOULD be placed on the token within children. External resource links, related artifact references, and internal relationships. References: presentationImage, richText, presentationVideo, presentationCode, presentationUrl, status, token, tokenGroup, tokenGuideline, link, extensions

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification

GitHub
